description_long: This is a large, double-headed sledgehammer with a robust cylindrical handle and two massive, spiked striking heads on either end. The heads are adorned with gold-colored bands and feature prominent, evenly spaced spikes, giving the hammer a menacing and reinforced appearance. The central shaft is wrapped in alternating gold and white segments, suggesting a reinforced grip and decorative design. The handle is straight and appears to be constructed for heavy-duty use, with additional spikes protruding near the head for extra impact or intimidation. The overall construction suggests a tool designed for both function and visual impact, possibly for ceremonial or specialized demolition purposes. The surface finish is smooth with metallic and painted elements, and there are no visible signs of wear or branding.
description: A large, double-headed sledgehammer with spiked, gold-banded heads and a reinforced, straight handle. The design is both functional and visually striking, with smooth metallic and painted surfaces.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full width of the double spiked heads and the straight, segmented handle with visible gold bands.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the circular profile of the hammer head and the pointed spikes radiating outward, with the handle appearing narrow.
description_view_2: Rear view mirrors the front, displaying the symmetrical double heads and the central handle with spikes.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the end of the hammer head and the alignment of the spikes.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical handle, likely wood, wrapped with alternating gold and white painted segments for grip enhancement.
attribute_head_type: Double sledgehammer head with cylindrical shape, each end featuring multiple outward-facing spikes and gold-colored bands.
